questions.questionText: Tubuloglomerular feedback is mediated primarily by sensing of sodium chloride at the:
questions.explanation: Macula densa cells of the juxtaglomerular apparatus sense tubular NaCl delivery and regulate afferent arteriolar tone and renin release, mediating tubuloglomerular feedback. The proximal tubule and thin limb do not perform this signaling role. Collecting duct is important for final urine concentration, not JGA sensing.

questions.questionText: The tubular maximum concept is best applied clinically to:
questions.explanation: Glucose is freely filtered and reabsorbed up to a transport maximum in the proximal tubule; beyond this threshold glycosuria appears. Urea handling is not described by a simple transport maximum in the same exam context. Water excretion is hormonally regulated. Sodium filtration depends on GFR, not tubular maximum.

questions.questionText: A patient with SIADH is expected to have which urinary finding?
questions.optionD: High bicarbonate with low sodium excretion
questions.explanation: In SIADH, excess ADH causes increased water reabsorption in the collecting duct, producing inappropriately concentrated urine despite hypo-osmolality of plasma. Dilute urine is expected when ADH is suppressed, as in primary polydipsia. Glucosuria and bicarbonaturia are unrelated here.
questions.source: Guyton and Hall, Renal Regulation of Osmolality
